source_text_snapshot:
A cascade connection of two voltage amplifiers \( A_1 \) and \( A_2 \) is shown in the figure. The open-loop gain \( A_{v0} \), input resistance \( R_{in} \), and output resistance \( R_o \) for \( A_1 \) and \( A_2 \) are as follows:

\[
\begin{aligned}
& A_1 = A_{v0} = 10 \quad R_{in} = 10 \text{ k}\Omega \quad R_o = 1 \text{ k}\Omega \\
& A_2 = A_{v0} = 5 \quad R_{in} = 5 \text{ k}\Omega \quad R_o = 200 \Omega
\end{aligned}
\]

The approximate overall voltage gain \( \frac{v_{out}}{v_{in}} \) is \(\qquad\)

source_text_snapshot: In the chopper circuit shown in <image>, the main thyristor \(T_M\) is operated at a duty ratio of 0.8 which is much larger than the commutation interval. If the maximum allowable reapplied \( \frac{dV}{dt} \) on \( T_M \) is \(50 \text{ V}/\mu\text{s}\), what should be the theoretical minimum value of \(C_1\)? Assume current ripple through \(L_0\) to be negligible.
normalized_text_snapshot: In the chopper circuit shown in the figure, the main thyristor \(T_M\) is operated at a duty ratio of 0.8, which is much larger than the commutation interval. If the maximum allowable reapplied \(\frac{dV}{dt}\) on \(T_M\) is \(50 \, \text{V}/\mu\text{s}\), what should be the theoretical minimum value of \(C_1\)? Assume current ripple through \(L_0\) to be negligible.
